decision: Extract security subsystem into optional plugin (flowspec-security)
rationale: Security dominates 37% of commands, 55% of skills, 23% of CLI source. Most sessions don't need it. Extraction reduces core by ~21,000 lines.

decision: Migrate slash commands to unified skills system with YAML frontmatter
rationale: Claude Code v2.1.3 unified skills system replaces the command/skill distinction. Skills support context: fork, auto-invocation, and disable-model-invocation.

decision: Merge /flow:build into /flow:implement
rationale: Build is a sub-phase of implement, rarely invoked standalone. Eliminating saves 149 lines and reduces command count.

decision: Merge /flow:pre-pr and /flow:submit-n-watch-pr into pr-lifecycle closed-loop skill
rationale: Pre-PR always precedes submit. The submit command is already a model-driven retry loop. Combining creates a single closed-loop skill (~176 lines).

decision: Convert /flow:gate to auto-invoked skill
rationale: Gate is a pre-implementation check never typed by users. Auto-invocation is the correct trigger mechanism.

decision: Convert /flow:rigor to hook + auto-invoked skill
rationale: Rigor should auto-trigger on git operations, not require manual invocation.

decision: Eliminate constitution-checker skill, replace with hook
rationale: Duplicates constitution-check partial. Hook-based approach has zero prompt cost and deterministic trigger.

decision: Eliminate workflow-executor skill
rationale: Duplicates /flow:custom command. One mechanism for custom workflows is sufficient.

decision: Eliminate _rigor-rules.md partial (1,278 lines)
rationale: Move validation logic to CLI code (rigor.py already exists). Eliminates largest single partial from context budget.

decision: Eliminate _constitution-check.md partial (161 lines)
rationale: Replaced by constitution-checker hook per D-007.

decision: Refactor /flow:specify to reference pm-planner skill instead of inlining persona
rationale: Specify command contains ~250 lines of inline PM Planner persona that duplicates the pm-planner skill content.

decision: Refactor /flow:plan to reference architect skill instead of inlining persona
rationale: Plan command contains ~316 lines of inline Architect/Platform personas that duplicate the architect skill.

decision: Merge sdd-methodology skill into rules
rationale: Content overlaps with CLAUDE.md and workflow YAML. Rules are always-loaded and more appropriate for methodology constants.

decision: Merge context-extractor skill into pm-planner
rationale: Context-extractor is only invoked by other commands, never independently. pm-planner is the natural host.

decision: Create closed-loop-tester skill
rationale: Autonomous test-fix-verify cycle (max 3 iterations) eliminates the #1 pain point: manual retry of failing tests.

decision: Create code-quality-validator closed-loop skill
rationale: Lint -> fix -> format -> verify cycle as autonomous skill reduces manual formatting iteration.

decision: Create pr-lifecycle closed-loop skill
rationale: Create PR -> monitor CI -> fix failures -> resubmit. Replaces manual PR management workflow.

decision: Create git-operations auto-invoked skill
rationale: Consolidates branch naming, worktree management, DCO sign-off, and rebase guidance into one skill.

decision: Create codebase-mapper auto-invoked skill
rationale: Converts /flow:map-codebase from user-invoked command to auto-invoked skill for planning context.

decision: Replace extended thinking triggers with adaptive thinking guidance
rationale: budget_tokens is deprecated in March 2026. Adaptive thinking handles depth automatically. Remove think/think hard/megathink/ultrathink directives.

decision: Structure prompts as Input/Process/Output/Constraints contracts
rationale: Universal prompt structure works across models. Replaces Claude-specific persona prose with functional role descriptions.

decision: Keep Claude-specific XML structuring as default format
rationale: Claude is the primary platform. XML tags work well for Claude. Model-agnostic means content quality, not platform portability. Don't build adaptation infra without a second real target.

decision: Use context: fork for heavy skills (security, architecture)
rationale: Heavy skills pollute the main context window. context: fork isolates them into separate context, preserving working memory for code analysis.

decision: Package flowspec as distributable Claude Code plugin
rationale: Plugin marketplace has 9,000+ plugins. Distribution via marketplace enables organic discovery and 1-command install.

decision: Phase migration: simplification FIRST, model-adaptive SECOND, new features THIRD
rationale: Scope creep is the #1 premortem risk. Each phase must deliver independent value. 6-week timebox for Phase 1.

decision: Preserve institutional knowledge before touching prompts
rationale: Prompts contain baked-in lessons (e.g., Copilot re-review behavior, test deletion history). Extract rationale into separate docs.

decision: Define flowspec identity as methodology toolkit, not developer tool suite
rationale: Competing with Claude Code built-ins is unwinnable. SDD methodology (assess/specify/plan workflow, structured artifact generation, quality gates) is the differentiator.

decision: Keep /flow:* namespace for backward compatibility
rationale: Existing users and documentation reference /flow:* commands. Migration script maps old -> new but preserves the namespace.

decision: Implement progressive context loading with tiers
rationale: Context engineering > prompt engineering. Only load what the current phase needs. Reduces framework overhead from 3,500-4,000 lines to ~1,000-1,500 per phase.

decision: Keep security.md rule (96 lines) in core for general coding standards
rationale: General secure coding practices (no hardcoded secrets, input validation, injection prevention) apply to all projects, not just security-focused work.

finding: Skills system unified in v2.1.3 with YAML frontmatter, context: fork, and disable-model-invocation support
Opus 4.6 removes prefill support; adaptive thinking replaces budget_tokens
50 Python files, 11,493 lines in security CLI subsystem
23 command files totaling 6,756 lines; 5 security commands (2,525 lines)
22 skill files totaling 8,737 lines; 12 core security skills plus 3 supplementary
4 partials totaling 1,861 lines; _rigor-rules.md alone is 1,278 lines (69% of partial content)
Framework overhead per /flow:implement invocation: 3,500-4,000 lines (15-25% of 200K context window)
Automated change deleted ~1,560 lines of tests in single commit. Established non-negotiable test deletion rule.
